mission.title: spire: Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a registrar to self-identify
mission.description: Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a registrar to self-identify. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a registrar to self-identify

steps.title: Check current Namespace configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire resources:
```bash
kubectl get namespace -A
kubectl describe namespace <name> -n <namespace>
```
We've discussed this before but I failed to find the issue so I thought I'd open a new one to keep the conversation going.

SPIRE Controller Manager tries to be authoritative for the workload entries

steps.title: Apply the fix for Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a registra
steps.description:
Only one external controller manager is supported at a time until
https://github.com/spiffe/spire/issues/4898 is resolved.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Only one external controller manager is supported at a time until
https://github.com/spiffe/spire/issues/4898 is resolved.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
